**Note:** The slight variation between $106.97 (page description/metadata, likely the settlement or earlier snapshot) and $107.25 (live commodities table) reflects intraday price movement. The chart metadata "last" of $106.6584 may represent a slightly earlier intraday print.

### Key Statistics

- **Previous Day Close:** $105.02/bbl
- **Daily Change:** +$2.23 / +2.13% (from live table), or +1.86% (from description snapshot at $106.97)

### WTI Crude Oil (for context)
- **WTI Crude (CL1:COM):** $100.81/bbl (live table, May 21)
- **WTI Daily Change:** +$2.551 / +2.60%
- **WTI earlier snapshot:** $100.44/bbl
- **Brent-WTI Spread:** ~$6.44/bbl

### Front-Month Contract Details

The Trading Economics ticker **CO1:COM** tracks the **front-month Brent crude oil futures contract** (ICE Brent). Given the typical expiry schedule for ICE Brent futures (the front-month contract expires approximately on the last business day of the month, two months prior to the delivery month), the front-month contract as of May 21, 2026 is most likely the **July 2026 contract**.

### Market Context & News (May 21, 2026)

1. **Brent crude futures stabilized around $105/bbl earlier on Thursday (May 21)** after falling nearly 6% over the previous two sessions, then rebounded above $106-$107.

2. **US-Iran negotiations** are a dominant driver: President Trump stated the US is nearing the "final phase" of negotiations with Iran. Iran is reviewing the latest US proposal. A potential deal could reopen the **Strait of Hormuz**, where tanker traffic has been disrupted since March.

3. **Iran announced** the creation of a "Persian Gulf Strait Authority" with a "controlled maritime zone" in the Strait of Hormuz — adding uncertainty.

4. **Oil prices remain ~50% above pre-war levels**, supported by supply tightness and US inventory drawdowns.

5. **US Strategic Petroleum Reserve:** The US withdrew nearly **10 million barrels** from the SPR last week — described as the **largest single release on record**.

6. **Supply timeline:** Abu Dhabi National Oil Co. (ADNOC) CEO said **full recovery in Middle Eastern oil flows is unlikely before late 2027**.

7. **Satellite data** showed three supertankers passing through the Strait of Hormuz, providing some supply optimism, though analysts noted Persian Gulf shipments take ~2 months to reach end markets.

### Previous Sessions (from news stream)
- **May 20, 2026:** Brent rose above $106/bbl, but held most of a nearly 6% prior drop
- **May 19, 2026:** Brent fell more than 5% to the $105/bbl level on hopes of Middle East supply restoration

### Sources

- **TradingEconomics — Brent Crude Oil:** https://tradingeconomics.com/commodity/brent-crude-oil (accessed May 21, 2026)
- **TradingEconomics — Crude Oil (WTI):** https://tradingeconomics.com/commodity/crude-oil (accessed May 21, 2026)
